rounds.name: Round 4 · 句法骨架
rounds.description: 整块从句 / 介词短语 / 修饰结构

tokens.v: Claire says the government should step in to protect consumers, Andrew says

tokens.answer: it should step aside so business can grow.

tokens.v: I think both views skip over

tokens.answer: what actually makes either side possible.

tokens.v: The most useful role of government is to build the common base — roads, schools, power grids, and basic research —

tokens.answer: that no single company will pay for but every company needs in order to work.

tokens.v: Each of the two views collapses without this base. Rules that protect buyers only help

tokens.answer: if buyers are educated enough to read a label or understand a loan,

tokens.v: and education is public. Business freedom only works

tokens.answer: where roads, courts, and electricity already exist,

tokens.v:
since no company builds those by itself.
Silicon Valley did not come from less regulation. It came from

tokens.answer: decades of federal money spent at Stanford and DARPA that produced the internet, GPS, and the transistor.

tokens.v:
None of these replaced the market; they built the stage the market performs on.
Rules and freedom are smaller knobs. The deepest one — the one with the widest reach —

tokens.answer: is whether the government builds what markets cannot build for themselves.
